text: Apollo 11 lands the first humans on the Moon.
title: Apollo 11

text: The Berlin Wall falls, opening the border between East and West Germany.
title: Berlin Wall

text: The Wright brothers make the first powered, controlled flight of an aeroplane.
title: Wright Flyer

text: Alexander Fleming discovers penicillin, opening the era of antibiotics.
title: Penicillin

text: Johannes Gutenberg introduces movable-type printing to Europe.
title: Printing press

text: Tim Berners-Lee proposes the World Wide Web at CERN.
title: World Wide Web

text: The double-helix structure of DNA is first described.
title: Nucleic acid double helix

text: The storming of the Bastille ignites the French Revolution.
title: Storming of the Bastille

text: The Thirteenth Amendment abolishes slavery throughout the United States.
title: Thirteenth Amendment to the United States Constitution

text: The Hubble Space Telescope is launched into low Earth orbit.
title: Hubble Space Telescope

text: Magna Carta is sealed, limiting the power of the English crown.
title: Magna Carta

text: The Soviet Union launches Sputnik 1, the first artificial satellite.
title: Sputnik 1

text: Rosa Parks refuses to give up her bus seat in Montgomery, Alabama.
title: Rosa Parks

text: The United Nations is founded in the aftermath of the Second World War.
title: United Nations
